animeOriginsCodes.title: Anime Origins Codes
animeOriginsCodes.subtitle: Track active Anime Origins codes and the official channels that announce new rewards.
animeOriginsCodes.intro: Anime Origins codes can grant launch rewards, summoning resources, and other progression items. New codes are published through the official Anime Origins Discord and announced across the game's community channels.

items.reward: No public Anime Origins reward codes are currently available.
items.requirements: Check the official Discord announcements for the latest code drops.

items.reward: No official expired-code archive has been published yet.
items.requirements: Redeem every code quickly because Roblox codes are case-sensitive and can expire.

items.reward: Launch, update, and community milestone rewards are announced through the Anime Origins Discord.
items.requirements: Join the server and follow its announcement and update channels.

items.reward: Rewards are added directly to the matching in-game balance or inventory.
items.requirements: Launch Anime Origins, open the code entry interface, paste the full code, and confirm the redemption.

tips: Copy codes exactly as written — Roblox codes are case-sensitive.
Redeem new codes as soon as they drop before they expire.
Watch the official Discord for launch and milestone reward drops.
If a code fails, double-check spelling and capitalization before trying again.

animeOriginsBeginnerGuide.title: Anime Origins Beginner Guide
animeOriginsBeginnerGuide.subtitle: Learn the summoning, team-building, and tower-defense loop before entering harder modes.
animeOriginsBeginnerGuide.intro: Anime Origins is an anime tower-defense game built around collecting units, placing them along enemy routes, and upgrading them during battle. Story Mode is the best starting point before moving into Infinite Mode, raids, and other challenges.

steps.title: Summon Your First Units
steps.description: Use the summoning system to obtain units for your starting roster. Build a usable team before spending resources on duplicate or highly specialized units.
steps.tip: Start with units that can cover both grouped enemies and stronger single targets.

steps.title: Build a Balanced Team
steps.description: Choose units with different attack ranges, placement costs, and combat roles. A balanced starter team needs reliable damage and enough coverage for crowded waves.
steps.tip: Avoid filling every slot with units that do the same job.

steps.title: Begin with Story Mode
steps.description: Use Story Mode to learn maps, enemy routes, and the normal wave structure. Clearing early stages also provides the progression needed to unlock and improve more units.
steps.tip: Push into new stages before repeatedly farming the easiest map.

steps.title: Place Units Near Long Paths
steps.description: Deploy units where enemies stay inside their attack range for the longest time. Corners, bends, and overlapping sections let the same unit attack more often.
steps.tip: Check a unit's range circle before confirming its placement.

steps.title: Upgrade During Each Match
steps.description: Spend battle currency on the units already covering the most important part of the route. Upgrades improve their combat performance and help them handle later waves.
steps.tip: Strengthen a small core group before placing too many low-level units.

steps.title: Improve and Evolve Your Roster
steps.description: Use progression resources to upgrade, evolve, and unlock stronger versions of useful units. Focus long-term investment on units that stay effective across multiple modes.
steps.tip: Save valuable evolution materials for units that will remain in your main team.

steps.title: Enter Infinite Mode and Raids
steps.description: Move into Infinite Mode, raids, and extra challenges after your Story team becomes consistent. These modes test sustained damage, boss performance, and team coordination.
steps.tip: Free private servers are great for practicing strategies or playing with chosen teammates.

quickTips: Always confirm a unit's range before placing it on the map.
Upgrade a core group of units instead of spreading currency too thin.
Save evolution materials for units you plan to use long-term.
Use free private servers to practice harder strategies risk-free.

animeOriginsUnitTierList.title: Anime Origins Unit Tier List
animeOriginsUnitTierList.subtitle: Compare the strongest publicly showcased Anime Origins units by rarity, summon value, and expected combat role.
animeOriginsUnitTierList.intro: The highest-priority units are rare characters with dedicated summon cutscenes, strong attack showcases, or valuable endgame potential. Rankings consider damage, range, attack speed, area coverage, and performance against bosses.

units.name: Starrk
strengths: Featured in an official summoning preview

units.name: Range and Attack Speed
units.role: How consistently a unit can attack

units.name: Mode Value
units.role: Performance in Story, Infinite, and Raids

animeOriginsUnitsAndSummoning.title: Anime Origins Units and Summoning
animeOriginsUnitsAndSummoning.subtitle: Browse confirmed units, known summon information, and featured character previews.
animeOriginsUnitsAndSummoning.intro: Units are obtained through the Anime Origins summoning system and used to defend routes against enemy waves. Rarity, summon probability, attack type, and upgrade potential determine how valuable each pull is.

items.name: Yuta Okkotsu
items.details: Yuta has a dedicated secret summon cutscene and is one of the rarest publicly shown Anime Origins units.

items.name: Starrk
items.details: Starrk appears in the game's summoning and unit sneak-peek footage.

items.name: Meliodas (Assault Mode)
items.details: Assault Mode Meliodas has a dedicated showcase displaying his attacks and upgrade progression.

items.name: Standard Banner Units
items.details: Regular banners provide the main pool of units used to build early and progression teams.

items.name: Featured Banner Units
items.details: Featured banners highlight selected units and should be checked before spending summoning resources.

items.name: Secret Units
items.details: Secret units use special summon presentations and have far lower acquisition rates than regular banner characters.

animeOriginsTraitsAndRerolls.title: Anime Origins Traits and Rerolls
animeOriginsTraitsAndRerolls.subtitle: Compare the main trait bonuses and decide which units deserve your limited rerolls.
animeOriginsTraitsAndRerolls.intro: Traits improve a unit's combat performance by changing stats such as damage, attack cooldown, and range. Save rerolls for units that will stay in your main team, especially rare damage dealers and strong evolved units.

items.combatEffect: Increases the damage dealt by each attack
items.bestFor: Primary damage dealers, boss killers, and high base-damage units
items.usageTip: A strong pick for units that already have reliable range and attack speed.

items.combatEffect: Reduces the delay between attacks and raises total damage over time
items.bestFor: Fast attackers and units with powerful repeated abilities
items.usageTip: Prioritize cooldown when more frequent attacks improve both damage and crowd control.

items.combatEffect: Expands the area a unit can target from its placed position
items.bestFor: Hill units, map-wide attackers, and units placed near long enemy paths
items.usageTip: Range shines on maps where one placement can cover several turns or lanes.

items.combatEffect: Improves more than one core combat stat instead of specializing in one area
items.bestFor: Flexible units used across Story, Infinite, and raids
items.usageTip: Keep balanced traits on versatile units unless you are chasing a stronger premium roll.

items.combatEffect: A top-rarity trait roll with a unique activation cutscene
items.bestFor: Your strongest long-term carry or evolved unit
items.usageTip: The showcased Secret Trait has a 0.1% roll rate, making it the main chase result when optimizing an endgame unit.

items.combatEffect: Replaces the selected unit's current trait with another roll
items.bestFor: Mythic, Secret, and other permanent team units
items.usageTip: Do not spend large numbers of rerolls on temporary starter units. Build a usable team first, then optimize one main carry at a time.

animeOriginsEvolutionGuide.title: Anime Origins Evolution Guide
animeOriginsEvolutionGuide.subtitle: Turn eligible units into stronger forms with upgraded attacks, abilities, and combat stats.
animeOriginsEvolutionGuide.intro: Evolution is a permanent unit upgrade for characters with an available advanced form. Check every requirement before committing materials, and prioritize evolutions that strengthen a unit already used in your main Story, Infinite, or raid team.

steps.name: Obtain an Eligible Unit
steps.action: Acquire a unit that has an available evolved form.
steps.details: Eligible characters show an evolution route through the unit progression system. Regular units without an evolution entry cannot use this feature.

steps.name: Review the Evolution Requirements
steps.action: Open the evolution interface and inspect the full material list.
steps.details: Requirements are tied to the selected character and must be collected before the upgrade can be completed.

steps.name: Collect Evolution Materials
steps.action: Clear progression content and repeat the modes that drop the required resources.
steps.details: Story stages, Infinite Mode, raids, and other challenges form the main loop for earning upgrade resources.

steps.name: Prepare the Correct Unit
steps.action: Select the exact unit copy you want to evolve.
steps.details: Use the unit intended for your permanent team and confirm it has the trait and build you want to keep developing.

steps.name: Complete the Evolution
steps.action: Submit the required materials through the evolution menu.
steps.details: The original character converts into its advanced form and becomes available for placement as the upgraded unit.

steps.name: Test the Evolved Form
steps.action: Compare its placement cost, upgrade path, attack type, and ability behavior.
steps.details: Evolved forms deliver stronger combat performance through improved stats, enhanced attacks, or additional abilities.

steps.name: Build Around the Upgrade
steps.action: Pair the evolved unit with support, economy, and crowd-control characters.
steps.details: Use the unit's improved role to shape your Story, Infinite, and raid teams instead of filling every slot with damage dealers.

steps.name: Advanced Form Showcase
steps.action: Meliodas in Assault Mode has been publicly showcased as an advanced combat form.
steps.details: The showcase demonstrates the upgraded model, attack effects, and ability presentation used for high-level units.

animeOriginsStoryInfiniteAndRaids.title: Anime Origins Story Mode, Infinite Mode and Raids
animeOriginsStoryInfiniteAndRaids.subtitle: Choose the right team and placement strategy for each major Anime Origins challenge.
animeOriginsStoryInfiniteAndRaids.intro: Anime Origins combines stage-based progression with endless wave defense and harder raid encounters. A reliable team normally needs one main damage dealer, early-wave coverage, crowd control or support, and enough economy to complete expensive upgrades.

modes.objective: Clear structured stages and protect the base through every required wave.
modes.recommendedTeam: Early-wave attacker, main damage dealer, support or crowd-control unit, and an economy option
modes.placementTip: Place inexpensive coverage near the first useful turn, then save central high-coverage spots for your strongest units.
modes.rewardFocus: Account progression, stage completion rewards, and unit-strengthening resources
modes.bestFor: New players and unlocking later content

modes.objective: Survive continuously scaling waves for as long as your team can hold the route.
modes.recommendedTeam: Scalable damage, wide-area attacks, reliable support, and units that stay strong after max upgrades
modes.placementTip: Use positions that cover multiple sections of the path and leave room for late-wave damage dealers.
modes.rewardFocus: Repeatable progression resources and rewards based on deeper wave clears
modes.bestFor: Resource farming and testing endgame teams

modes.objective: Defeat a harder encounter built around stronger enemies and demanding wave patterns.
modes.recommendedTeam: High single-target damage, area damage, support effects, and units matched to the raid's pressure
modes.placementTip: Coordinate upgrade timing with teammates and avoid spending all starting resources on one expensive unit.
modes.rewardFocus: High-value progression rewards and resources tied to advanced unit upgrades

modes.objective: Team up with other players to combine unit roles and defend the same route.
modes.recommendedTeam: Split responsibilities between early defense, economy, support, and late-game damage
modes.placementTip: Avoid overlapping identical coverage when another player can protect a different lane.
modes.rewardFocus: Faster and more consistent clears of difficult content
modes.bestFor: Friends, coordinated farming, and difficult stages

modes.objective: Practice placements, test upgrades, or organize controlled farming sessions.
modes.recommendedTeam: Any experimental lineup built around the mode being tested
modes.placementTip: Use repeated runs to compare where each unit reaches the most enemies and when each upgrade becomes affordable.
modes.rewardFocus: Strategy testing and organized group play
modes.bestFor: Learning maps and refining team rotations

modes.objective: Play the same tower-defense progression across supported Roblox devices.
modes.recommendedTeam: Use a simple placement order when playing on smaller touchscreens
modes.placementTip: Zoom out before placing key units and confirm the selected character before buying an upgrade.
modes.rewardFocus: Full access to the core progression loop
modes.bestFor: Mobile, tablet, PC, and console players

animeOriginsUpdates.title: Anime Origins Updates
animeOriginsUpdates.subtitle: Follow the latest publicly shown units, traits, modes, and gameplay systems.
animeOriginsUpdates.intro: Anime Origins updates are shared through the official Discord and supporting gameplay channels. The reveals below cover the confirmed gameplay loop, major modes, rare unit showcases, and high-rarity progression systems.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Anime Origins

questions.question: What is Anime Origins?
questions.answer: Anime Origins is an upcoming Roblox anime tower defense game developed by SNAP GAMES. You summon anime units, upgrade and evolve them, and defend against waves across multiple modes.

questions.question: What game modes does Anime Origins have?
questions.answer: Anime Origins features Story Mode, Infinite Mode, and Raids as its core modes, with additional challenges and events planned for future updates.

questions.question: What platforms can I play Anime Origins on?
questions.answer: Anime Origins is a Roblox game, so it runs on Mobile, Tablet, PC, and Console wherever Roblox is supported. Private Servers are free.

questions.question: When will Anime Origins release?
questions.answer: Anime Origins is currently in the Upcoming stage and has not officially launched yet. Follow the official Discord for the latest release date and beta announcements.

questions.question: How do summons and banners work in Anime Origins?
questions.answer: Summons let you roll for anime units on the banner using in-game currency. Higher-rarity units have lower drop rates, and a pity system guarantees a featured unit after a set number of pulls.

questions.question: Where can I get Anime Origins codes?
questions.answer: New Anime Origins codes are released by the developers through the official Discord and social channels. Redeem them in-game for free gems, coins, and exclusive items.

cta.title: Ready to Play Anime Origins?
